title: Google India Reimagined
subtitle: Innovating Google's core search for India's evolving digital landscape.
problem_statement: Despite Google's dominance, a gap exists for hyper-localized, context-aware search experiences in India.

approach.goal: Understand primary user needs for hyper-local, vernacular search in India.
approach.method_desc: Best for new market entry and understanding cultural nuances.

pillars.title: Information Overload
pillars.insight: Users struggle filtering vast global information for local relevance.

pillars.title: Language Barrier
pillars.insight: Many prefer searching in native languages, not just English.

pillars.title: Digital Divide
pillars.insight: Access to relevant, localized content varies greatly by region.

artifacts.label: Strategy
artifacts.title: Defining the Challenges
questions: How might we personalize search for diverse Indian users?
How might users discover local, relevant information easily?
How might we integrate vernacular content seamlessly?

personas.name: Rohan Sharma
personas.quote: I need quick, local business info in Hindi.
personas.bio: Rohan seeks local market prices, government schemes, and supplier contacts for his shop.

personas.name: Priya Singh
personas.quote: I want trending topics and study resources fast.
personas.bio: Priya uses search for academic research, current events, and entertainment in English/Marathi.

insights: "Where can I find local train schedules?"
"Is this information relevant to my city?"
"Wish I could just speak my query."

points.action: Geo-fenced search results based on user location.
Local business directory integration with reviews.
Contextual news feed for specific regions.
Enhanced voice search for all Indian languages.
Automatic language detection and result filtering.
Transliteration tools within the search bar.
Verified local expert contributions for answers.
Fact-checking for regional news and information.

pivot.before: Generic global search results often lack local relevance for Indian users.
pivot.pivot_logic: Shift from a global-first to a hyper-local, vernacular-first search paradigm for India.
pivot.after: Personalized, culturally relevant search experience empowering every Indian.

annotations.title: Vernacular Input
annotations.rationale: Allows typing/speaking in multiple Indian languages.

annotations.title: Smart Suggestions
annotations.rationale: Offers predictive text in local languages.

data.finding: System status is clear, but local relevance feedback is missing.
Consistent branding and layout across Google products.
Search bar prevents basic input errors effectively.
Offers basic customization (language), but limited advanced options.
